refs.title: A Comprehensive Overview of Large Language Models
refs.why: Exhaustive 2024 survey covering LLM architecture, training, fine-tuning, and context length improvements; 1000+ citations.

refs.title: Attention Is All You Need
refs.why: The original Transformer paper introducing the architecture that all modern LLMs are built on; 100k+ citations, NeurIPS 2017.

refs.title: Scaling Laws for Neural Language Models
refs.why: Established the empirical scaling laws showing how LLM performance improves with model size, data size, and compute; 9000+ citations.

refs.title: Neural Machine Translation of Rare Words with Subword Units
refs.why: Introduced BPE tokenization for neural networks, the foundation of most LLM tokenizers (GPT, LLaMA, etc.); 10k+ citations.

refs.title: Formalizing BPE Tokenization
refs.why: Rigorous formal treatment of BPE as used in SentencePiece and HuggingFace tokenizers, clarifying semantics and edge cases.

refs.title: HuggingFace Tokenizers Documentation — Models (BPE)
refs.why: Official documentation from the most widely used tokenization library, with complete BPE/WordPiece/Unigram model specs and code examples.

refs.title: Attention Is All You Need
refs.why: Defined the standard Transformer with fixed-length self-attention, establishing the architectural basis for context windows; 100k+ citations.

refs.title: A Survey of Techniques to Extend the Context Length in Large Language Models
refs.why: Comprehensive 2024 survey cataloging RoPE extensions, ALiBi, position interpolation, and long-context methods across major LLMs.

refs.title: LongRoPE: Extending LLM Context Window Beyond 2 Million Tokens
refs.why: State-of-the-art method extending LLaMA2 context to 2M+ tokens with non-uniform position interpolation; demonstrates practical limits.

refs.title: Efficient Memory Management for Large Language Model Serving with PagedAttention (vLLM)
refs.why: Introduces PagedAttention and vLLM, the most widely adopted open-source LLM inference engine; SOSP 2023.

refs.title: vLLM Official Documentation
refs.why: Official docs for the leading open-source LLM serving engine, detailing continuous batching, PagedAttention, and production deployment.

refs.title: llama.cpp — LLM inference in C/C++
refs.why: The most popular open-source inference engine for local/on-device LLM deployment; 115k+ stars, supports CPU/GPU/quantized inference.

refs.title: SGLang — Official Repository
refs.why: High-performance serving framework with RadixAttention prefix caching, used in production; from LMSys.

refs.title: Scaling Laws for Neural Language Models
refs.why: Definitive paper showing LLM loss scales as power-law with parameter count; directly explains why model size (parameters) matters; 9000+ citations.

refs.title: Attention Is All You Need
refs.why: Original Transformer defining all weight matrices (Q, K, V, FFN) that constitute the bulk of LLM parameters; 100k+ citations.

refs.title: The Super Weight in Large Language Models
refs.why: Recent analysis showing that a tiny fraction of parameters disproportionately affect LLM output; provides modern perspective on weight significance.

refs.title: The Curious Case of Neural Text Degeneration (Nucleus Sampling / top-p)
refs.why: Introduced top-p (nucleus) sampling, the standard decoding method used by GPT-4, Claude, and most LLMs; 3000+ citations, ICLR 2020.

refs.title: Hierarchical Neural Story Generation (top-k sampling)
refs.why: Introduced top-k sampling for language generation, now a standard API parameter in OpenAI, Anthropic, and Google LLM services; ACL 2018.

refs.title: A Systematic Characterization of Sampling Algorithms for Open-ended Language Generation
refs.why: Systematic quality-diversity trade-off analysis of top-k, nucleus (top-p), and tempered sampling; AACL 2020.

refs.title: Top-nσ: Not All Logits Are You Need
refs.why: Modern analysis of logit structure showing Gaussian-distributed noise vs informative logits; introduces statistical thresholding directly on pre-softmax logits.

refs.title: Logits Are All We Need to Adapt Closed Models
refs.why: Demonstrates how access to raw logits enables adaptation techniques beyond prompt engineering, explaining their central role in LLM interfaces.
